He replied: 'The one questioned about it is no more knowledgeable than the questioner—but I will tell you of its tokens (أشراطها).'

'When the slave-girl gives birth to her mistress, that is among its tokens. When the barefoot and naked are the shepherds of sheep and the leaders of the people, that is among its tokens. And when the shepherds of sheep compete in constructing tall buildings, that is among its tokens.'"

In al-‘Ilal, with its chain to Anas ibn Malik from the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) in a long hadith in which he says to Abdullah ibn Salam, who had asked him about several matters: "As for the tokens of the Hour (أشراط الساعة)—it is a fire that will gather the people from the East to the West."

I say: Perhaps what is meant by it is other than its outward meaning. The narrations regarding the tokens of the Hour from the paths of the Shi‘a and the Sunnis are beyond counting. The narration of Salman from the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) and the narration of Humran from al-Sadiq (عليه‌السلام) have already passed at the end of the fifth volume of this book, and they are two comprehensive narrations on this subject.

In al-Majma‘, the hadith is authentic with the chain to Hudhayfah ibn al-Yaman, who said: "I was a man with a sharp tongue toward my family—so I said: 'O Messenger of Allah, I fear that my tongue will enter me into the Fire.' The Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) said: 'Where are you regarding the seeking of forgiveness (الاستغفار)? Indeed, I seek forgiveness from Allah a hundred times a day.'"

In al-Durr al-Manthur, Ahmad, Ibn Abi Shaybah, Muslim, Abu Dawud, al-Nasa’i, Ibn Hibban, and Ibn Mardawayh narrated from al-Agharr al-Muzani, who said: "The Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) said: 'Indeed, my heart becomes clouded (ليغان على قلبي), and indeed I seek forgiveness from Allah every day a hundred times.'"

In the same source, regarding His saying, the Exalted: فَهَلْ عَسَيْتُمْ إِنْ تَوَلَّيْتُمْ ("May it not be that if you were to wield authority...") the verse: al-Bayhaqi narrated from Jabir ibn Abdullah, who said: "The Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) said: 'Indeed, the womb (الرحم) is suspended from the Throne (العرش); it has an eloquent tongue saying: O Allah, join the one who joins me, and sever the one who severs me.'"

I say: The narrations regarding it [the womb/kinship] and its joining and severing are many, and a portion of them has passed in the commentary on the beginning of Surah al-Nisa’.

In al-Majma‘, regarding His saying, the Exalted: أَفَلا يَتَدَبَّرُونَ الْقُرْآنَ ("Do they not contemplate the Quran...") the verse: "Do they not contemplate the Quran so that they may fulfill the truth that is upon them?" Narrated from Abu Abdillah and Abu al-Hasan (عليهما السلام).

In al-Tawhid, with its chain to Muhammad ibn ‘Umarah, who said: "I asked al-Sadiq Ja‘far ibn Muhammad (عليه‌السلام), saying to him: 'O son of the Messenger of Allah, inform me about Allah, the Mighty and Majestic—does He have pleasure (رضى) and displeasure (سخط)?' He replied: 'Yes—but that is not like what is found in created beings. Rather, the anger (غضب) of Allah is His punishment, and His pleasure (رضاه) is His reward.'"

In al-Majma‘, regarding His saying, the Exalted: وَلَتَعْرِفَنَّهُمْ فِي لَحْنِ الْقَوْلِ ("and you will surely recognize them by their tone of speech") the verse: from Abu Sa‘id al-Khudri, who said: "The 'tone of speech' was their hatred for Ali ibn Abi Talib." He said: "We used to recognize the hypocrites during the time of the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) by their hatred for Ali ibn Abi Talib."

The author of al-Majma‘ said: "The like of that has been narrated from Jabir ibn Abdullah al-Ansari."

He said: "And from ‘Ubadah ibn al-Samit, who said: 'We used to test our children with the love of Ali ibn Abi Talib—so if we saw one of them who did not love him, we knew he was not of sound birth (لغير رشدة).'"

In al-Durr al-Manthur, Ibn Mardawayh narrated from Ibn Mas‘ud, who said: "We did not recognize the hypocrites during the time of the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) except by their hatred for Ali ibn Abi Talib."

In the Amali of al-Tusi, with its chain to Ali (عليه‌السلام), that he said: "I said four things for which Allah, the Exalted, sent down confirmation in His Book. I said: 'A man is hidden under his tongue; when he speaks, he appears.' So Allah sent down: وَلَتَعْرِفَنَّهُمْ فِي لَحْنِ الْقَوْلِ ('and you will surely recognize them by their tone of speech')."

Ayahs

۞ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ أَطِيعُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُوا۟ ٱلرَّسُولَ وَلَا تُبْطِلُوٓا۟ أَعْمَـٰلَكُمْ (٣٣)
O you who have faith! Obey Allah and obey the Apostle, and do not render your works void. (33)
إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ وَصَدُّوا۟ عَن سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ ثُمَّ مَاتُوا۟ وَهُمْ كُفَّارٌ فَلَن يَغْفِرَ ٱللَّهُ لَهُمْ (٣٤)
Indeed those who are faithless and bar from the way of Allah and then die faithless, Allah will never forgive them. (34)
فَلَا تَهِنُوا۟ وَتَدْعُوٓا۟ إِلَى ٱلسَّلْمِ وَأَنتُمُ ٱلْأَعْلَوْنَ وَٱللَّهُ مَعَكُمْ وَلَن يَتِرَكُمْ أَعْمَـٰلَكُمْ (٣٥)
So do not slacken and [do not] call for peace when you have the upper hand and Allah is with you, and He will not stint [the reward of] your works. (35)
إِنَّمَا ٱلْحَيَوٰةُ ٱلدُّنْيَا لَعِبٌ وَلَهْوٌ ۚ وَإِن تُؤْمِنُوا۟ وَتَتَّقُوا۟ يُؤْتِكُمْ أُجُورَكُمْ وَلَا يَسْـَٔلْكُمْ أَمْوَٰلَكُمْ (٣٦)
The life of the world is just play and diversion, but if you are faithful and Godwary, He will give you your rewards, and will not ask your wealth [in return] from you. (36)
إِن يَسْـَٔلْكُمُوهَا فَيُحْفِكُمْ تَبْخَلُوا۟ وَيُخْرِجْ أَضْغَـٰنَكُمْ (٣٧)
Should He ask it from you and press you, you will be stingy, and He will expose your spite. (37)
هَـٰٓأَنتُمْ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ تُدْعَوْنَ لِتُنفِقُوا۟ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ فَمِنكُم مَّن يَبْخَلُ ۖ وَمَن يَبْخَلْ فَإِنَّمَا يَبْخَلُ عَن نَّفْسِهِۦ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ ٱلْغَنِىُّ وَأَنتُمُ ٱلْفُقَرَآءُ ۚ وَإِن تَتَوَلَّوْا۟ يَسْتَبْدِلْ قَوْمًا غَيْرَكُمْ ثُمَّ لَا يَكُونُوٓا۟ أَمْثَـٰلَكُم (٣٨)
Ah! There you are, being invited to spend in the way of Allah; yet among you there are those who are stingy; and whoever is stingy is stingy only to himself. Allah is the All-sufficient, and you are all-needy, and if you turn away He will replace you with another people, and they will not be like you. (38)

(Commentary)

After describing the state of the disbelievers (الكفار) and adding to it the description of those in whose hearts is a sickness (الذين في قلوبهم مرض) and their sluggishness regarding the matter of fighting, as well as the state of those among them who apostatized afterward, He returns to warn the believers (المؤمنين) against becoming like them.

He warns them against negotiating with the polytheists (المشركين) or inclining toward them, lest they follow what angers Allah and detest His pleasure, thereby nullifying their deeds through frustration (الحبط) — the total loss of the spiritual reward and efficacy of one's actions due to subsequent sin or disbelief. These verses contain an exhortation for them through encouragement and intimidation, and through the instilling of hope and fear; with this, the Surah concludes.

His saying, the Exalted: يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَطِيعُوا اللهَ وَأَطِيعُوا الرَّسُولَ وَلا تُبْطِلُوا أَعْمالَكُمْ "O you who have faith! Obey Allah and obey the Apostle, and do not render your deeds void" (47:33). Although this verse is independent in its indication and absolute in its meaning—to the extent that jurists (الفقهاء) have used His saying: وَلا تُبْطِلُوا أَعْمالَكُمْ "and do not render your deeds void" as evidence for the prohibition of invalidating prayer after starting it—it must be viewed in light of its occurrence within the context (السياق) of the preceding verses dealing with the matter of fighting (القتال). This is also true for the subsequent verses that follow the same context, especially what appears in the outward sense of His saying: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا "Indeed those who disbelieve..." etc., as a justification (التعليل), and what is in His saying: فَلا تَهِنُوا وَتَدْعُوا إِلَى السَّلْمِ "So do not flag and [do not] plead for peace..." etc., as a derivation (التفريع).

In short, the verse, in view of its context, indicates the obligation of obeying Allah, the Glorified, in what He has sent down of the Book and legislated of rulings, and the obligation of obeying the Messenger in what he conveys from Allah, the Glorified, and in the commands he issues by virtue of his authority (الولاية) over the believers within the religious society. It also warns the believers against nullifying their deeds by performing what necessitates the frustration of deeds (حبط أعمالهم), as was the case with those of weak faith who inclined toward hypocrisy (النفاق), some of whom eventually apostatized after guidance (الهدى) — the divine leading toward truth and the straight path — had become clear to them.

Thus, according to the specific occasion, the intended meaning of "obeying Allah" is obeying Him in what He legislated and sent down regarding the ruling of fighting; "obeying the Messenger" is obeying him in what he conveyed thereof and what he commanded regarding it and its preliminaries by virtue of his authority (الولاية) therein; and "rendering deeds void" is failing to comply with the ruling of fighting, just as the hypocrites and the people of apostasy (أهل الردة) failed.

It has been said that the intended meaning of "rendering deeds void" is their frustration (إحباطها) by acting as if they have done a favor (المن) for Allah and His Messenger by their faith, as in His saying, the Exalted: يَمُنُّونَ عَلَيْكَ أَنْ أَسْلَمُوا "They count it as a favor to you that they have submitted" (49:17). It has also been said: nullifying them through ostentation (الرياء) and seeking reputation (السمعة); or through vanity (العجب); or through disbelief (الكفر) and hypocrisy (النفاق). It has also been said: the intended meaning is nullifying charities (الصدقات) through reminders of generosity and injury, as He said: لا تُبْطِلُوا صَدَقاتِكُمْ بِالْمَنِّ وَالْأَذى "do not render your charities void by reminders of your generosity and by injury" (2:264). It has also been said: nullifying them through acts of disobedience (المعاصي), or specifically through major sins (الكبائر).

The response to all these views is that each one of them—assuming its correctness and acceptance—is a particular instance (مصداق) among the instances of the verse, if one ignores its occurrence in the context (السياق) previously mentioned. However, in terms of its occurrence in the context, it does not encompass anything other than fighting (القتال), as previously stated.

His saying, the Exalted: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَصَدُّوا عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللهِ ثُمَّ ماتُوا وَهُمْ كُفَّارٌ فَلَنْ يَغْفِرَ اللهُ لَهُمْ "Indeed those who disbelieve and bar from the way of Allah, and then die as faithless, Allah will never forgive them" (47:34). The apparent context is that this is a justification (تعليل) for the content of the previous verse. It implies that if you do not obey Allah and the Messenger and you nullify your deeds by following what angers Allah and detesting His pleasure, it will lead you to join the people of disbelief and barring [from His way], and there is no forgiveness for them after dying in such a state, ever.

The intended meaning of "barring from the way of Allah" (الصد عن سبيل الله) is turning away from faith or preventing people from believing.

His saying, the Exalted: فَلا تَهِنُوا وَتَدْعُوا إِلَى السَّلْمِ وَأَنْتُمُ الْأَعْلَوْنَ وَاللهُ مَعَكُمْ وَلَنْ يَتِرَكُمْ أَعْمالَكُمْ "So do not flag and [do not] plead for peace, for you are the upper hand and Allah is with you, and He will not shortchange you in your deeds" (47:35). This is a derivation (تفريع) from what preceded. His saying: فَلا تَهِنُوا "So do not flag" is from الوهن (weakness/flagging), meaning weakness and lethargy. His saying: وَتَدْعُوا إِلَى السَّلْمِ "and [do not] plead for peace" is conjoined to "flag" (تهنوا) and falls under the scope of the prohibition; i.e., "and do not plead for peace." السلم (peace)—with a fatha on the sin—means reconciliation (الصلح). His saying: وَأَنْتُمُ الْأَعْلَوْنَ "for you are the upper hand" is a circumstantial clause (جملة حالية), meaning: do not seek reconciliation while the situation is that you are the victors (الغالبون). The intended meaning of "height" (العلو) is victory (الغلبة), which is a well-known metaphor (استعارة).

His saying: وَاللهُ مَعَكُمْ "and Allah is with you" is conjoined to "for you are the upper hand," explaining the reason for their superiority and justifying it. The intended meaning of His being "with them" is the accompaniment of support (معية النصر), rather than the ontological accompaniment (المعية القيومية) referred to in His saying, the Exalted: وَهُوَ مَعَكُمْ أَيْنَ ما كُنْتُمْ "and He is with you wherever you may be" (57:4).

Regarding His saying: وَلَنْ يَتِرَكُمْ أَعْمالَكُمْ "and He will not shortchange you in your deeds," it is said in al-Majma': It is said: وتره يتره وترا when he diminishes it for him; from this is the hadith: "as if he were deprived (وتر) of his family and wealth." Its root is "cutting" (القطع), and from it comes الترة (the cutting off [of life] by killing), and الوتر (the odd number), which is cut off by its isolation from others. [End of quote].

The meaning is: He will not diminish your deeds for you; i.e., He will pay their reward in full and complete. It has also been said the meaning is: He will not let your deeds go to waste; or: He will not wrong you. These meanings are close to one another.

The meaning of the verse is: Since the path of not obeying Allah and the Messenger and nullifying your deeds is this path, and it leads to being deprived of Allah's forgiveness forever, then do not weaken or flag in the matter of fighting, and do not invite the polytheists to reconciliation and the cessation of fighting while you are the victors and Allah is your helper against them; He will not diminish anything of your rewards, but will pay them to you in full and complete.

In the verse is a promise to the believers of victory and triumph if they obey Allah and the Messenger; thus, it is like His saying: وَلا تَهِنُوا وَلا تَحْزَنُوا وَأَنْتُمُ الْأَعْلَوْنَ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ "Do not iron out and do not grieve, for you shall have the upper hand, should you be faithful" (3:139).

His saying, the Exalted: إِنَّمَا الْحَياةُ الدُّنْيا لَعِبٌ وَلَهْوٌ وَإِنْ تُؤْمِنُوا وَتَتَّقُوا يُؤْتِكُمْ أُجُورَكُمْ وَلا يَسْئَلْكُمْ أَمْوالَكُمْ "The life of the world is just play and diversion, but if you have faith and are God-conscious, He will give you your rewards, and He will not ask your wealth [of] you" (47:36). This is an encouragement for them toward the Hereafter and an inducement to renounce the world by explaining its reality: that it is play (لعب) and diversion (لهو) — the meaning of its being play and diversion has already been discussed.

His saying: وَإِنْ تُؤْمِنُوا "but if you have faith..." etc., means that if you believe and are God-conscious (تتقوا) — possessing the godwariness that protects the soul from Allah's displeasure — through obeying Him and obeying His Messenger, He will give you your rewards and will not ask you for your wealth in exchange for what He has given you. The apparent context is that "wealth" (الأموال) refers to all of their wealth, which is also supported by the following verse.

His saying, the Exalted: إِنْ يَسْئَلْكُمُوها فَيُحْفِكُمْ تَبْخَلُوا وَيُخْرِجْ أَضْغانَكُمْ "Should He ask them of you and press you, you will be stingy, and He will expose your grudges" (47:37). الإحفاء (pressing/exacting) means exhausting and imposing hardship. The intended meaning of stinginess (البخل)—as has been said—is withholding from giving, and الأضغان (grudges) are deep-seated hatreds (الأحقاد).

The meaning is: If He were to ask you for all your wealth and exhaust you by demanding all of it, you would withhold from giving due to your love for it, and He would bring out the grudges of your hearts, and thus you would go astray.

His saying, the Exalted: ها أَنْتُمْ هؤُلاءِ تُدْعَوْنَ لِتُنْفِقُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ فَمِنْكُمْ مَنْ يَبْخَلُ "Ah! There you are, being invited to spend in the way of Allah; yet among you are those who are stingy..." (47:38) to the end of the verse, is in the position of providing evidence for the explanation of the previous verse. It is as if it were said: If He were to ask for everything and press you, you would be stingy; the evidence for this is that here you are, being invited to spend in the way of Allah—which is only a portion of your wealth—yet some of you are stingy. Thus, it becomes apparent that if He were to ask all of you for all [your wealth], you would all be stingy.

His saying: وَمَنْ يَبْخَلْ فَإِنَّما يَبْخَلُ عَنْ نَفْسِهِ "and whoever is stingy is stingy only to himself" (47:38); i.e., he withholds good from himself. For Allah does not ask for their wealth to benefit from it Himself, but rather so that the spenders may benefit from it in what contains the good of their worldly life and their Hereafter. Thus, their refusal to spend it is a refusal of good for themselves. To this, He points in His subsequent saying: وَاللهُ الْغَنِيُّ وَأَنْتُمُ الْفُقَراءُ "and Allah is the All-sufficient, and you are the needy" (47:38). The two instances of restriction (القصران) are for reversal (القلب); i.e., Allah is the One who is All-sufficient (الغني), not you, and you are the needy (الفقراء), not Allah.

His saying: وَإِنْ تَتَوَلَّوْا يَسْتَبْدِلْ قَوْماً غَيْرَكُمْ ثُمَّ لا يَكُونُوا أَمْثالَكُمْ "and if you turn away, He will replace you with another people, and they will not be like you" (47:38). It has been said: this is conjoined to His saying: وَإِنْ تُؤْمِنُوا وَتَتَّقُوا "but if you have faith and are God-conscious." The meaning is: if you believe and are God-conscious, He will give you your rewards; and if you turn away and disregard [the command], He will replace you with another people by granting them the success (التوفيق) to believe instead of you, and then they will not be like you; rather, they will believe, be God-conscious, and spend in the way of Allah.

(A Discussion of Narrations)

In Thawab al-A'mal, from Abu Ja'far (peace be upon him), who said: The Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: من قال : سبحان الله غرس الله له بها شجرة في الجنة ، ومن قال : الحمد لله غرس الله له بها شجرة في الجنة ، ومن قال : لا إله إلا الله غرس الله له بها شجرة في الجنة ، ومن قال : الله أكبر غرس الله له بها شجرة في الجنة. — "Whoever says: 'Glory be to Allah' (سبحان الله), Allah plants for him a tree in Paradise because of it. Whoever says: 'Praise be to Allah' (الحمد لله), Allah plants for him a tree in Paradise because of it. Whoever says: 'There is no god but Allah' (لا إله إلا الله), Allah plants for him a tree in Paradise because of it. And whoever says: 'Allah is the Greatest' (الله أكبر), Allah plants for him a tree in Paradise because of it."

A man from the Quraysh said: "O Messenger of Allah, then our trees in Paradise must be many!" He replied: نعم ولكن إياكم أن ترسلوا عليها نارا فتحرقوها ، وذلك أن الله عز وجل يقول : « يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَطِيعُوا اللهَ وَأَطِيعُوا الرَّسُولَ وَلا تُبْطِلُوا أَعْمالَكُمْ » — "Yes, but beware of sending a fire upon them and burning them; that is because Allah, Mighty and Majestic, says: 'O you who have faith! Obey Allah and obey the Apostle, and do not render your deeds void.'"

In the Tafsir of al-Qummi, regarding: وَإِنْ جَنَحُوا لِلسَّلْمِ فَاجْنَحْ لَها "And if they incline toward peace, then you [too] incline toward it" (8:61), he said: "It is abrogated (منسوخة) by His saying: فَلا تَهِنُوا وَتَدْعُوا إِلَى السَّلْمِ وَأَنْتُمُ الْأَعْلَوْنَ وَاللهُ مَعَكُمْ 'So do not flag and [do not] plead for peace, for you are the upper hand and Allah is with you.'"

In al-Durr al-Manthur, Abd al-Razzaq, Abd ibn Humayd, al-Tirmidhi, Ibn Jarir, Ibn Abi Hatim, al-Tabarani in al-Awsat, and al-Bayhaqi in al-Dala'il narrated from Abu Hurayrah, who said: The Messenger of Allah recited this verse: وَإِنْ تَتَوَلَّوْا يَسْتَبْدِلْ قَوْماً غَيْرَكُمْ ثُمَّ لا يَكُونُوا أَمْثالَكُمْ "and if you turn away, He will replace you with another people, and they will not be like you." They asked: "O Messenger of Allah, who are these people who, if we turn away, will be replaced with us?" The Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) struck the shoulder of Salman and then said: هذا وقومه ، والذي نفسي بيده لو كان الإيمان منوطا بالثريا لتناوله رجال من فارس. — "This one and his people. By the One in whose hand is my soul, if faith were suspended from the Pleiades, men from Persia would surely attain it."

I say: It has been narrated through other chains from Abu Hurayrah similarly. Likewise, from Ibn Mardawayh from Jabir similarly.

In al-Majma', Abu Basir narrated from Abu Ja'far (peace be upon him) that he said: إِنْ تَتَوَلَّوْا "and if you turn away"—O assembly of Arabs—يَسْتَبْدِلْ قَوْماً غَيْرَكُمْ "He will replace you with another people"—meaning the non-Arabs (الموالي).

In the same source, from Abu Abdillah (peace be upon him), who said: قد والله أبدل خيرا منهم الموالي. — "By Allah, He has already replaced [them] with those better than them: the non-Arabs (الموالي)."
